title: jacquemontia ruderaria
intro: Jacquemontia ruderaria, also known as the country devil or rough bindweed, is a perennial vine native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Jacquemontia ruderaria include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other herbs, to create teas and infusions for medicinal purposes.
